title: Action Figure Display Shelf
summary: This simple to build shelf is a great way to decrease the toy clutter by getting your kids’ favorite action figures (in this case, Pokémon) off of the ground and displayed neatly.
description: This simple to build shelf is a great way to decrease the toy clutter by getting your kids’ favorite action figures (in this case, Pokémon) off of the ground and displayed neatly. Both the dimensions and the theme of the shelf can be easily customized to best house the particular action figure collection that’s taken over your home.

materials.name: Box of 1 ¼ pocket hole screws (see tools list)
Spray paint (in your choice of color)

steps.title: Cut wood
steps.body:
Cut all shelf boards to size on your miter saw as per cut list or your specific requirements.

**Tip:** OPTIONAL: In this case, the action figures mount on small round discs for display purposes. I measured out the correct spacing on each shelf and, using a drill press, countersunk a space for each figure to fit into.

steps.title: Sand
steps.body: Using a random orbital sander, sand all of your pieces smooth.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes
steps.body: Using your favorite Kreg Pocket-Hole Jig (I used the 720PRO) drill 2 holes at each end of your top and bottom pieces as well as your shelves.

steps.title: Paint
steps.body: This step can be considered optional, but if you really want the shelf to fit the theme of your kids’ favorite characters, spray paint the individual shelf pieces with the appropriate colors (in this case, red, black, and white).

steps.title: Assemble shelf
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom pieces to the side boards using 1-1/4” pocket hole screws. Once that’s done, attach your shelves evenly spaced (in this case the shelves are spaced 3.5” apart.

steps.title: Cut MDF backer
steps.body:
Using your circular saw or table saw, cut your 1/8” MDF backer to fit the back of your shelf. In this case, I cut the backer to 12” x 15-1/2”.

**Tip:** OPTIONAL: Spray paint your MDF backer to fit the theme of your shelf.

steps.title: Attach MDF backer
steps.body: Using a brad nailer and 1” brad nails, attach your MDF backer board to the back of your shelf.

steps.title: Organize
steps.body:
Have your kids get those action figures off of the ground and organized neatly on their new shelf.

**Tip:** OPTIONAL ACRYLIC DOORS: Take your shelf one step further by cutting some 1/8” clear acrylic to size and attaching with hinges to keep the toys in and the dust out.
